channel.title: Blog Posts on Connected-Boxes
channel.description: Recent content in Blog Posts on Connected-Boxes

item.title: Post#3: Long Weekends and Digital Homes: Why I Swapped LinkedIn for Hugo
item.description:
This long weekend, I finally scratched an itch I’ve had for years: starting a personal blog.

For a long time, I relied on LinkedIn to share my thoughts. But LinkedIn has a “version control” problem. Recently, I tried to edit an image in a post and watched the entire layout implode with no way to recover the old version. That was the final straw. I needed a digital home I actually owned—a place where I could break things, fix them, and keep a history of it all.

item.title: Post#2: What's Scam Prevention Framework
item.description:
Scams are real, and they’re everywhere#

Who hasn’t heard of scams? Many of us have. For most of the lucky ones, it’s just news we’ve heard. It’s not close or personal. But they are very real - In Australia in 2024, people lost $2.7 billion of their hard-earned money to scams.

What exactly is a scam?#

Legally, scams and fraud are different terms. While fraud is broader, scams typically involve two scenarios:

item.title: Post#1: 25 Years Of Drawing Boxes
item.description:
My two daughters have distilled down my entire career down to a single sentence:

“Dad gets paid to talk and draw boxes.”

They aren’t wrong.

I have been very fortunate to live in the space between a problem and a solution. Usually that space is filled with thousands of whiteboard sessions and meetings, lot of coffees, mostly frantic deadlines and yes, many connected boxes.

I have realised that my daughter’s summation is actually the most grounded way to describe what I do. Its all about solving problems -
